sop_title: Duplicate | Blankets | Size Info:

scenarios.description: The size dimension in the product title is displayed in a reversed format (e.g., 86.6" x 78.74" vs 78.7 x 86.6 inch) between two products.

conditions.textual_evidence: The alphabetic value in the 'Size' column exactly matches (e.g., 'E').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The secondary image shows the exact same size dimensions in the same format for both items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Mark as Duplicate if the size column matches and the secondary image reflects the same dimensions, ignoring the reversed size string in the product title.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Food form:

scenarios.description: The 'Food Form' attribute has conflicting values (e.g., 'Liquids' vs 'Bites') for the same identifiable food product.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product names, packaging descriptions (e.g., '3 ct Pack, 44 oz Bottles'), and other primary identifiers are identical.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Main product images visually match, displaying the exact same item and quantity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Food Form' attribute and mark as Duplicate if the remaining product information and visual evidence match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Food Form :

scenarios.description: The 'Food Form' attribute differs (e.g., 'Cubes' vs 'Thins') for an identical snack/food product.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Name, Brand, Product Long Description, and other key metadata effectively match.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the same identical product box.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Food Form' attribute mismatch and base the Duplicate decision on the alignment of the remaining available information.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home improvements | Ignore Warranty:

scenarios.description: The 'Has Written Warranty' attribute indicates 'No' for one item and 'Yes - Warranty Text' for another, but the products are otherwise identical furniture pieces.

conditions.textual_evidence: All physical attributes, such as assembled product weight, length, load weight, and type, are exactly the same.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Main images show the exact same piece of furniture.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the warranty attribute for furniture items; if all other physical specifications and visual evidence match, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Ignore Pattern values

scenarios.description: The 'Pattern' attribute has distinct assigned values (e.g., 'Pattern 3' vs 'Pattern 12') for products that appear identical visually and in text.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product names, materials (e.g., Oxford cloth), shape, and specific dimensions match exactly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The printed pattern on the bag in the main image visually matches perfectly between the items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore generic pattern value mismatches if the visual representation of the pattern and all other product specifications are identical, and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Improvements | Ignore the Reference number:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Trailing alphabet characters/junk codes in Product Name

scenarios.description: Product names match exactly except for differing single trailing alphabetic characters or junk codes at the end of the title string.

conditions.textual_evidence: All core descriptive text in the Product Name matches, with only a differing letter (e.g., 'E', 'B', 'F', 'H') appended at the very end.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Main product images visually match, displaying the exact same item.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the trailing alphabetic junk codes in the Product Name. Mark as Duplicate if the remaining product title text and visual evidence match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Ignore Reference number:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Appended reference numbers in Product Name

scenarios.description: Product names contain differing reference numbers (e.g., D151, D54, D26) at the end of the title, but represent the exact same product.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product titles describe identical items with differing alphanumeric reference codes appended.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the identical fashion accessory or shoe insert across all items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the reference numbers given at the end of the title. If the rest of the attributes and images match, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: The product titles contain a mismatching or incorrect generic trailing word (like 'T-shirt' for a dress) at the very end, but the preceding text has enough relevant information to identify the product.

conditions.textual_evidence: The preceding bulk of the Product Name accurately describes the item (e.g., 'Short Sleeve Dress Womens Summer Dresses'), followed by an extraneous or conflicting term.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The product image clearly matches the primary description (e.g., showing a dress) and matches across comparing items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the trailing generic term (like 'T-shirt') if there is enough relevant matching information in the preceding text and images to confirm the items are the same. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: The distinct tabular attribute for 'Sleeve Length Style' contains conflicting data (e.g., 'Short Sleeve' vs 'Long Sleeve'), but the Product Name and Main Image explicitly confirm the actual style (e.g., both say/show 'Long Sleeve').

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name explicitly states the sleeve length (e.g., 'Long Sleeve') consistently across items, contradicting one of the tabular attribute values.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main images clearly show the exact same sleeve length, validating the Product Name rather than the conflicting attribute.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Sleeve Length Style' attribute mismatch if both the Product Name and the Main Image explicitly align and confirm the same feature. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: For one-size accessories like baseball caps, there are discrepancies in tabular attributes like 'Clothing Size Group' (e.g., Plus vs Regular vs Women's Plus) and 'Season' (Summer vs Fall vs Spring).

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name and main descriptive details match perfectly for an inherently standard-sized/seasonless item (like a basic plain baseball cap).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images show the exact same item (same style, color, cut).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore 'Clothing Size Group' and 'Season' attributes for standard accessories like baseball caps. If all other core information and images match, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Inseam:

scenarios.description: The 'Inseam' attribute contains explicitly different valid numerical values (e.g., 11 in vs 9).

conditions.textual_evidence: The inseam values provided in the attribute columns do not match.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual evidence is secondary; the explicit attribute difference drives the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the inseam values are different between the two products, mark them as Not a Duplicate.

scenarios.description: The 'Inseam' attribute is present for one product but missing (null) for the other.

conditions.textual_evidence: One side has an inseam value and the other side does not.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

resolution.actionable_instruction: If one side has an inseam value and the other side does not, mark it as Not Sure - Bad data.

scenarios.description: One product shows an inseam of 1 inch, while the other shows a standard value like 7 inches.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN#1 shows 1 inch and GTIN#2 shows 7 inches.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

resolution.actionable_instruction: Assume that the 1-inch inseam information is not available/invalid for GTIN#1 and mark the comparison as Not Sure - Bad Data.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Show width:

scenarios.description: For open footwear like sandals, slippers, or flip flops, there are discrepancies in tabular attributes like 'Shoe Width' (e.g., Narrow; Medium; Wide vs Wedge) or 'Shoe Closure'.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Shoe Width' or 'Shoe Closure' attributes show differing values for inherently open/slip-on footwear.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show the exact same identical sandal, slipper, or flip-flop.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore 'Shoe Width' and 'Shoe Closure' attributes for sandals, slippers, and flip flops. Base the decision on the visual matching of the images and other remaining core attributes.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: The 'Container Type' attribute differs (e.g., 'Jar' vs 'Box') for the identical food or beverage product.

conditions.textual_evidence: All other attributes like Brand (Watkins), Product Name, and Volume/Weight (19 oz) match perfectly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images show the exact same physical product and packaging (e.g., both show a jar).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Container Type' attribute discrepancy. Mark as Duplicate if the remaining attributes and visual evidence confirm they are the same product.

sop_title: Duplicate | Brand | Image:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Missing Brand Name on one side

scenarios.description: Brand name information is explicitly available in the data for one product (GTIN) but is missing or omitted in the data for the other product.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name and Short Description contain heavily overlapping, identical descriptive text and identifiers.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images for both items are identical, clearly showing the same branded product.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the brand name is present in one GTIN but missing in another, ignore the missing value and mark as Duplicate based on matching images and descriptions.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion Others | Ignoring the Hair Type :

scenarios.description: The 'Hair Type' attribute has mismatched values (e.g., 'Normal| Frizzy' vs 'All Hair Types') between products, but the descriptions indicate broad compatibility.

conditions.textual_evidence: The product description mentions 'All Hair Types' for both GTINs, overriding the specific attribute column mismatch.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images must show identically packaged items (e.g., exact same tube with same colors and text).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Hair Type' attribute if the product description states 'All Hair Types' for both and the visual packaging matches exactly. Mark as Duplicate.

scenarios.description: Products share similar text but have distinct visual packaging differences (e.g., one has a silver accent/cap, the other is solid black/purple).

conditions.textual_evidence: Product names and attributes may appear similar or identical.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images reveal clear packaging design differences between the items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If there is a clear packaging difference visible in the images, do not ignore it. Mark as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Home and Improvement | Rug Sizes:

scenarios.description: Product title, main image, and primary 'Size' attribute match, but a specific sub-attribute ('Rug Size') for one of the items presents conflicting dimensions.

conditions.textual_evidence: Primary 'Size' column shows '31.5" x 19.7"' for both, but 'Rug Size' for GTIN 2 shows '15.7x19.7x0.4', contradicting the title and primary size.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images appear identical (gray wavy rug), but textual dimensions strictly conflict.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Mark as 'Not sure - Bad data' when there is a strict size discrepancy between the title/general size attribute and a category-specific size attribute like 'Rug Size'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Occasion & Seasonal | Based on Actual Color:

scenarios.description: Both products share identical visual characteristics, titles, and 'Actual Color', while the generic 'Color' attribute is also matching but less specific.

conditions.textual_evidence: 'Actual Color' is 'Rose Gold' for both items. The generic 'Color' is 'Gold'. Priority is given to the 'Actual Color'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify that both images show the exact same color and pattern for the party plates and napkins.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Prioritize the 'Actual Color' attribute over the general 'Color' attribute. If 'Actual Color' matches, mark the items as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Shoes | Based on Shoe Size:

scenarios.description: Multiple shoe entries have variations or embedded sizes in their product titles (e.g., 'Size 8'), but the structured 'Shoe Size' attribute matches across all variants.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Shoe Size' attribute dictates the size ('46' for all), overriding any generic 'Size' column values or sizes embedded in the product title.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must display the identical shoe model and color (white slip-on flats).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Prioritize the 'Shoe Size' attribute. Even if there are differing values in the 'Size' column or title, if 'Shoe Size' matches identically, flag as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: Both products have a specific attribute ('Clothing Neck Style') that contains incorrect data ('V-Neck') which contradicts both the product title and the image ('Round Neck').

conditions.textual_evidence: Title explicitly states 'Round Neck', but 'Clothing Neck Style' attribute says 'V-Neck'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images clearly show a dress with a round neckline, confirming the attribute data is incorrect.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat the conflicting attribute ('Clothing Neck Style') as junk information. Since the image and title agree and match across both items, mark them as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Based on Clothing Size :

scenarios.description: One product title contains a specific size ('2t') that does not align with the structured clothing size, while both products share the exact same 'Clothing Size' attribute.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Name 2 contains '2t', but both items have '4-5 Years' in the 'Clothing Size' attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images display the identical purple long-sleeve dress.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore extraneous size details in the product title (e.g., '2t') and prioritize the structured 'Clothing Size' attribute. If 'Clothing Size' matches ('4-5 Years'), mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Toys | Ignoring the # Values :

scenarios.description: Products have identical names and images, but the 'Actual Color' attribute contains different system-generated hash suffixes appended to the same base color.

conditions.textual_evidence: 'Actual Color' shows 'Pink--#a' for one item and 'Pink--#b' for the other. The base color 'Pink' matches.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must show the exact same product in the exact same color (pink plush seat).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the trailing '#' values or system suffixes (e.g., '--#a', '--#b') in the 'Actual Color' attribute. If the base color name and images match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Improvement | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: Products share identical images and similar titles, but the 'Actual Color' attribute is populated with non-color style identifiers.

conditions.textual_evidence: 'Actual Color' contains values like 'Style 6' and 'A' instead of actual descriptive colors.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm both items are the exact same Hello Kitty soap dispenser design and color.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Actual Color' attribute entirely if it contains non-color values (e.g., generic style letters/numbers). Rely on the matching main image and product name to mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Occasional | Sets information :

scenarios.description: Products are multi-item sets with matching piece counts explicitly stated in the description, but explicit size attributes are missing for the items.

conditions.textual_evidence: The product description for both items explicitly mentions '102 pieces of'. There is no size information provided.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical assortments of party favor toys.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For products sold in sets, prioritize matching the set piece count found in the text/description. If the set piece count and images match, mark as 'Duplicate' even if individual size information is absent.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home improvement | Size / No size:

scenarios.description: A batch of visually identical products has a mix of populated 'Size' attributes and null 'Size' attributes.

conditions.textual_evidence: Some GTINs have '12 oz' in the Size column, while others have '-' (null/blank) in the Size column.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: All images show the identical blue tumbler.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Do not merge items with a populated size and items with a null size into the same cluster. Create Cluster 1 for all items with matching populated sizes (e.g., '12 oz') and Cluster 2 for all items with null sizes, marking them as duplicates within their respective distinct clusters.

sop_title: Duplicate | Animal | Size Discrepancy :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Size information embedded in Product Name

scenarios.description: Product titles contain conflicting unit measurements for size (likely a typo), but the manufacturer model number and secondary diagram images perfectly match.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title says '120X120X38CM' and the other says '120X120X38in'. However, the 'Model' attribute is identically '13331591' for both.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Secondary images contain explicit dimensional diagrams that match perfectly across both items, confirming the actual size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat size discrepancies in the product title (e.g., unit typos like CM vs in) as junk data if the 'Model' number strictly matches and the secondary images provide definitive proof of matching dimensions. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Academic Institution :

scenarios.description: The standard 'Clothing Size' attribute contains a generic size range, but a seemingly unrelated attribute ('Academic Institution') contains the specific, valid clothing size.

conditions.textual_evidence: Both items display 'S,M,L,XL' in 'Clothing Size', but have identical specific size 'XL' populated in the 'Academic Institution' attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must show the identical grey yoga set.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat valid size values found in the 'Academic Institution' attribute as the actual product size. Ignore the generic 'Clothing Size' range. If the misplaced sizes and images match, flag as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Everything Else | Ignore junk Info :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Trailing random numbers in Product Name

scenarios.description: Product titles contain varying random numeric suffixes, but the product description explicitly states the identical pack quantity for all items.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles vary with random numbers like 'Pad 4 4' or 'Pad 1 4', but the 'Product Short Description' confirms '4pcs' for all items.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the exact same set of 4 anti-vibration pads.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore varying trailing numbers in the product title (treat as junk data) when the product description provides clear, matching pack/quantity information. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Ignore Junk :

scenarios.description: Product titles are identical except for slightly differing alphanumeric codes appended at the end (likely SKUs or tracking IDs).

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles end in distinct codes such as 'Shirt-TN776' and 'Shirt-TH776', while the rest of the title string matches exactly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same white graphic t-shirt.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore minor alphanumeric code differences at the end of product titles as junk information. If the core title and main images match, flag as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Fashion - Other | Actual Color

scenarios.description: There is an internal contradiction within the product's attributes regarding color. The title and general color attribute indicate one color, while a component-specific color attribute indicates a different color.

conditions.textual_evidence: Title and 'Actual Color' state 'Light Brown', but the specific 'Watch Band Color' attribute contradicts this by stating 'Blue'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Image shows a watch with a brown band, which conflicts with the 'Watch Band Color' textual data.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Mark as 'Not sure - Bad data' due to color discrepancy when there is a strict contradiction between a specific attribute ('Watch Band Color') and the overall product title/'Actual Color'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home Improvement | Ignore Junk :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Minor inaccuracies in the structured Size attribute

scenarios.description: The structured 'Size' attribute has a slight typo/inaccuracy compared to the precise dimensions provided in the 'Product Short Description', but both items are clearly identical sets.

conditions.textual_evidence: Description shows accurate converted dimensions (e.g., '5.86X4.72X3.34in'), while the 'Size' attribute contains a near-similar typo (e.g., '5.8" x 4.7" x 13.3"'). Title confirms '4 Sets'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm both items are the exact same 4-piece spice container set.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the products are clearly identified as identical sets, consider minor discrepancies or typos in the structured 'Size' attribute as junk data, provided it is 'near to similar' to the accurate description size. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion - Others | Ignore Pant Rise :

scenarios.description: The 'Pant Rise' attribute is present for one item (e.g., 'High') but missing/blank for the other when the product type is shorts.

conditions.textual_evidence: The products are explicitly identified as shorts in the Product Name and other attributes.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show identical shorts.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Do not consider the 'Pant Rise' attribute for shorts products. Ignore the missing or mismatched value and flag as Duplicate based on remaining matching attributes and images.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Size from image

scenarios.description: The 'Size' tabular attribute is missing for one product, but the exact size is verifiable directly from the product packaging in the image.

conditions.textual_evidence: One GTIN has '16 oz' in the Size attribute and/or title, while the other GTIN is missing this data.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The product image clearly displays the weight/size (e.g., reading 'NET WT 16 OZ' on the packaging), matching the stated size of the other GTIN.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the size value is absent in the attribute fields for one GTIN, extract and consider the size information visible on the product image. Mark as Duplicate if it matches.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Two format size info :

scenarios.description: The 'Size' attribute and Product Names display mathematically equivalent volume/weight values but in different unit formats (e.g., Ounces vs Pounds).

conditions.textual_evidence: One item indicates '16 Oz' (or '1 lbs' in title) and the other indicates '1' in size and '1 lb' in title. 16 oz equals 1 lb.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images visually confirm the same identical bulk product.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Evaluate the size units mathematically. If the information given in 'Oz' is equivalent to the given 'lbs' value, treat the sizes as matching and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Books | Binding Type

scenarios.description: The 'Binding Type' attribute lists synonymous industry terms for the same physical binding format, specifically 'Case Binding' vs 'Hardback'.

conditions.textual_evidence: Binding Type shows 'Case Binding' for one GTIN and 'Hardback' for the other. Product titles may also confirm '(Hardcover)'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same physical book.

resolution.actionable_instruction: In book scenarios, consider 'Case Binding' and 'Hardback Binding' (or 'Hardback') as synonymous terms. Mark as Duplicate if other attributes match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Books | Bases on remaining Info :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Missing non-essential tabular attributes (e.g., Model, Variant Attribute Names, Character)

scenarios.description: Multiple secondary tabular attributes are completely missing or null for both books, but the core identifying information is present and matches perfectly.

conditions.textual_evidence: The primary available fields—Product Name, Publisher, Product Long Description, and Product Type—are identical across both GTINs.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images display the exact same book cover design.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If non-essential information is not available for validation, rely on the available core text fields and images. If the available core information matches perfectly, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Considering Image:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Bulk/Conflicting information in Clothing Neck Style

scenarios.description: One product has a clean 'Clothing Neck Style' value while the other contains bulk or conflicting values, but the primary images confirm the true style.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN 1 has 'Round Neckline' while GTIN 2 has 'V-Neck; Round Neckline' in the Clothing Neck Style attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must visually confirm that both sweaters have the exact same neck style (e.g., round neck), overriding the bulk text.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Prioritize the visual evidence in the main image over bulk or conflicting text in the 'Clothing Neck Style' attribute. If the images match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Toys | Age group

scenarios.description: Products are identical in imagery, name, and core details, and the rule specifically dictates ignoring age range recommendations for these items.

conditions.textual_evidence: Both products have '3 months' for Minimum Recommended Age and '6 months' for Maximum Recommended Age.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the exact same toy truck and sand tools set.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Minimum Recommended Age' and 'Maximum Recommended Age' attributes entirely when determining duplicates for Toys. If images and titles match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Books | Ignore fiction Info

scenarios.description: Book entries have identical titles, covers, and core details, but conflict on the 'Fiction/Nonfiction' classification attribute.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN 1 is marked 'Non-Fiction' and GTIN 2 is marked 'Fiction' in the 'Fiction/Nonfiction' attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the exact same book cover ('Long Live the Pumpkin Queen').

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore discrepancies in the 'Fiction/Nonfiction' attribute. If the book title, cover image, and other core identifiers match perfectly, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Ignore the Fabric Care instructions:

scenarios.description: Clothing items are visually identical and share the same core attributes and material, but have differing fabric care instructions.

conditions.textual_evidence: One item shows 'Hand Wash' while others show 'Machine Washable' in the Fabric Care Instructions attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the exact same pair of grey flare pants.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Fabric Care Instructions' attribute completely if all other main attributes (like material) and images match. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Improvement | Color:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Minor textual print/color discrepancies in Product Name

scenarios.description: Products have a textual print discrepancy in the title, but the primary image shares a significant visual color distribution (greater than 20%).

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles contain differing print descriptions ('Rose Flowers Print' vs 'Black Cats In A Garden Print').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show a dark bottle with a prominent white pattern. The shared pattern/color covers more than 20% of the item visually.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For these specific patterned items, if there is a textual color/print discrepancy but the primary images visually share more than 20% of the same color/pattern composition, classify them as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home Improvement | Ignore value in Material:

scenarios.description: The 'Material' attribute contains a clearly illogical or impossible value for the product type (e.g., 'Stainless Steel' for a soft fabric doormat/rug), while the other product has a realistic value (e.g., 'Polyester').

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name and Short Description describe the item as a soft flannel/fabric rug, making 'Stainless steel' an obvious data error.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the product is a standard fabric doormat with a printed character, not a metal product.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the clearly erroneous material value ('Stainless steel') for fabric items. Mark as Duplicate if remaining core attributes and images match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Furniture | Ignore Pillow Weight :

scenarios.description: Non-mandatory attributes like 'Weight' differ (e.g., 0.29 lbs vs 1.45 lbs), and there are minor material composition text variations (e.g., '100% Polyester' vs 'Cotton & Polyester blend') in the specifications for decorative pillows.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product titles, dimensions (18x18), and design themes (Funny Frog with Glasses) match perfectly, making weight and exact blend percentages secondary.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same printed decorative pillow design.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore non-mandatory attributes like 'Weight' and minor variations in material blend declarations (Polyester vs Cotton/Polyester blend) for pillows. Mark as Duplicate if the core design, dimensions, and images match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Ignore values in age group:

scenarios.description: The 'Age Group' tabular attribute shows conflicting values (e.g., 'Adult' vs 'Toddler'), but the Product Name explicitly defines the actual target age group (e.g., 'Toddler Baby Kids').

conditions.textual_evidence: Product titles clearly state 'Toddler Baby Kids Winter Boys Girls' for both items, proving the 'Adult' age group attribute is incorrect junk data.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical children's clothing items.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Totally ignore the 'Age Group' attribute mismatch if the Product Name clearly establishes the correct age group and all other information and images match. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Improvement | Ignore value in Model :

scenarios.description: The 'Model' attribute contains differing alphanumeric values, and the 'Size' tabular attribute contains junk codes (e.g., 'A2166' vs 'C6888') instead of actual dimensions.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Names contain the exact same dimensions ('20X30cm') and descriptive text, rendering the mismatched Model numbers and junk Size codes irrelevant.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same acrylic hangable shaving mirror.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore 'Model' number mismatches for mirrors. Also ignore junk alphanumeric codes in the 'Size' attribute if the actual dimensions match in the Product Name. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Shelf Life :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Specific shelf life numerical values (e.g., 360 vs 191 days)

scenarios.description: The specific numerical shelf life values mentioned in the description text (e.g., 'shelf life : 360' vs 'shelf life : 191') differ, but the overarching 'Food Condition' attribute is identical.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Food Condition' explicitly matches as 'Shelf-Stable' for both items. The Product Names and core details (Folgers Colombian, 12 count) match perfectly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical coffee pod boxes.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the overarching 'Food Condition' matches (e.g., both are Shelf-Stable), ignore discrepancies in the specific numerical shelf life values found in the descriptions and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Books | Ignore Dimensions:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Physical dimensions embedded in the product description

scenarios.description: Book entries share identical titles, cover images, and core description text, but have slightly different physical dimensions listed within the description text.

conditions.textual_evidence: One description specifies '[Size: 17.78 x 24.13 cms]' while the other specifies '[Size: 18.78 x 25.13 cms]'. All other textual data matches.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm the exact same book cover ('The Apostolic Bible Polyglot').

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore minor physical dimension discrepancies within the product descriptions for books. If the title, publisher, image, and other core info match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Sign Boards | Ignore model in Image

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Tiny model numbers printed directly on the product in the image

scenarios.description: Products have matching attributes and identical main visual designs, but the actual product images reveal differing model numbers printed in small text on the items themselves.

conditions.textual_evidence: All textual attributes match, but visual inspection shows 'OS-NS-18020' on one sign and 'OS-NS-18021' on the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify that the main graphic and text of the sign ('NOTICE REGULAR UNLEADED') is identical, ignoring the small part number in the corner.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore differing model numbers embedded visually within the product images for sign boards. If the core design and textual attributes match, process as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Decorations | Pack:

scenarios.description: The product titles explicitly state the correct pack quantity, while the structured count attributes contain the system default value of '1'.

conditions.textual_evidence: Both Product Names state '50 Pcs', but the 'Total Count' and 'Count Per Pack' attributes both incorrectly show '1'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must visually represent a bulk quantity matching the title (e.g., a multi-pack of bunny ear headbands).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Total Count' and 'Count Per Pack' attributes when they contain the default value '1' and the product title explicitly provides the true, matching pack size. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Automotive parts | Based on size

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Missing Model/Reference number in Product Name

scenarios.description: One product title contains a specific model number while the other does not, but both share identical, highly specific technical size descriptions and images.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN 1 title contains model 'ALL76142', GTIN 2 does not. However, both have matching specific descriptions: '6V to 36V - 1-1/4 in Diameter Hole - 3/8-16 in Threads'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must show the identical automotive part hardware.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the presence or absence of a model/reference number in the title if the products share identical, detailed size specifications in the description and matching images. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion | Sleeve Discrepancy :

scenarios.description: The structured sleeve attribute contains incorrect data that contradicts both the explicit product title and the visual evidence in the image.

conditions.textual_evidence: Both titles clearly state 'Long Sleeve', but the 'Sleeve Length Style' attribute incorrectly states 'Short Sleeve' for one of the items.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images clearly show the model wearing a long-sleeved top, confirming the title and contradicting the attribute.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Prioritize the visual image and the product title to determine sleeve length. Treat the conflicting 'Sleeve Length Style' attribute as junk data. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Arts & Crafts | Based on remaining values :

scenarios.description: For artificial flower products, the 'Plant Name' and 'Size' attributes may be missing, mismatched, or deemed non-mandatory, while the visual and textual descriptions align.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Names describe the same type of artificial/fake silk flowers.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show the identical artificial floral arrangement (e.g., orange rose bundles).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Plant Name' attribute and treat 'Size' as non-mandatory for artificial flowers. Mark as Duplicate if the main images and remaining descriptions match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Furniture | Shape attribute

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Variations in descriptive Product Name strings

scenarios.description: Product titles are highly varied and cluttered, but the core tabular attributes for 'Shape' and 'Size' are identical across the compared items.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Shape' attribute (e.g., Rectangle) and 'Size' attribute (e.g., 60" x 104") match exactly, overriding chaotic or messy title strings.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same tablecloth pattern and setup.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If explicit size and shape information is available and matches perfectly across the items, along with matching images, mark as Duplicate despite variations in the title.

sop_title: Duplicate | Everything Else | No size Info :

scenarios.description: The explicit tabular 'Size' attribute is missing for both products, but an identical model number is provided within the product description.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Product Short Description' contains an exact matching model number (e.g., 'model WHA931') for both items, and titles match.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show the identical piece of wall art.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the size info is missing but an identical model number is found in the product descriptions alongside matching images and titles, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Fashion - Others | Ignore clothing length style :

scenarios.description: The tabular attribute for 'Clothing Length Style' contains conflicting values (e.g., 'Short' vs 'Long'), but the visual evidence strongly confirms they are the same product.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product titles and types denote the same sleepwear item, despite one title potentially containing contradictory keywords.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the exact same garment with the exact same length (e.g., both images show a short nightgown).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Clothing Length Style' attribute mismatch. If all remaining core attributes and visual evidence from the images confirm the items are identical, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Packaging Different :

scenarios.description: The products are exactly the same in terms of brand, formula, flavor, and weight, but the manufacturer has updated the packaging design, resulting in differing images.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name confirms the exact same contents (e.g., IAMS Proactive Health Healthy Kitten Chicken, 7 lb bag) across both items.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images show the same brand and product line, but with a different bag design or layout (old vs. new packaging).

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the textual content confirms the exact same product, flavor, brand, and size, ignore minor packaging design/artwork differences in the images and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Academic institution :

scenarios.description: The actual clothing size value (e.g., 'XL') is mistakenly populated in the 'Academic Institution' attribute field rather than the expected 'Clothing Size' field.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Academic Institution' field consistently holds the specific size value across both items, while the standard 'Clothing Size' field may hold a broader or generic string (e.g., 'S,M,L,XL').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images visually match, displaying the exact same clothing set.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the size can be accurately identified from the 'Academic Institution' attribute and matches across items, ignore discrepancies in the standard 'Clothing Size' field and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home | Ignore Material :

scenarios.description: The 'Material' tabular attribute contains conflicting data (e.g., 'Stainless Steel' vs 'PVC'), but the Product Name explicitly confirms the correct material for both items.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name text explicitly and identically states the material (e.g., 'Never Rust Stainless Steel') for both products.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images display the exact same product, corroborating the title.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Material' attribute column values if the Product Name explicitly and consistently provides the material information. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Books | Reading Level :

scenarios.description: The products are identical books, but the 'Reading Level' attribute may cause unnecessary strictness or is deemed non-essential for deduplication of the title.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name, Series, and primary identifying text for the book match perfectly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show the identical book cover artwork.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore the 'Reading Level' attribute in book products. If the core title, cover image, and description match, mark them as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Electronics | TV Mount Stands:

scenarios.description: The 'Maximum Screen Size' attribute column contains differing or null values (e.g., 65 in, 43 in, null, 55 in), but the Product Name clearly defines the supported capacity range.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name explicitly states the minimum to maximum stand capacity (e.g., '19 to 43 inch TVs') which contradicts or overrides the single value in the attribute column.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images show the exact same physical TV mount stand.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the capacity range stated in the title differs from the 'Maximum Screen Size' attribute column, ignore the attribute column and base the decision on the title match. Mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home and Improvement | Ounce :

scenarios.description: The 'Volume Capacity' attribute column has incorrect or mismatching values (e.g., 12.5 oz vs 16 oz), but the core text fields heavily establish the correct volume.

conditions.textual_evidence: The explicit 'Ounce' value (e.g., '12 oz') is prominently and consistently present in the Product Name and Product Short Description for both items.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical product packaging and quantities (e.g., same stack of cups).

resolution.actionable_instruction: If 'Ounce' is present and matches in the title/description but 'Volume Capacity' differs, ignore 'Volume Capacity'. Rely on the text information and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Backpack Size:

scenarios.description: The explicit size attribute is missing for backpacks, but the product description details the capacity or compartment layout, and all other core attributes match.

conditions.textual_evidence: Size attribute is not present, but 'Product Long Description' explicitly mentions 'Large capacity' and 'multiple compartments'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must clearly show the identical backpack model (e.g., LED display backpack).

resolution.actionable_instruction: For backpacks with missing size data, check the description for capacity or compartment information. If this information is present and matches along with other attributes, mark as 'Duplicate'. (If both size and capacity are missing, mark as 'Not Sure - Bad Data').

sop_title: Duplicate | Automotive | Vehicle Parts:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Keywords PRT, IRT, RAP, TRI in Manufacturer Part Number

scenarios.description: Manufacturer Part Numbers (MPNs) differ only by specific trailing supplier/vendor keywords, while the base model number matches the title and other items.

conditions.textual_evidence: MPNs are '20/902901-IRT', '20/902901-RAP', and '20/902901-TRI'. The base number '20/902901' matches exactly across all items and aligns with the Product Name.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must show identical sets of hardware (e.g., O-rings and seals).

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore specific suffix keywords (PRT, IRT, RAP, TRI) in the Manufacturer Part Number. If the remaining base model number matches across items and aligns with the Product Name, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Size:

scenarios.description: Apparel items have matching core sizes and images, but differ or contain redundant info in secondary fit/demographic attributes.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Clothing Fit' differs ('Modern Fit' vs 'Regular'), but the primary 'Clothing Size' ('L') matches perfectly.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must display the identical apparel item (e.g., black tank top on the same model).

resolution.actionable_instruction: For apparel, explicitly ignore the 'Clothing Fit', 'Clothing Size Group', and 'Age Group' attributes. If the primary 'Clothing Size' and visual images match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Automotive | Model Number:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Trailing alphanumeric codes in Product Name

scenarios.description: Automotive parts have perfectly matching Manufacturer Part Numbers and Size attributes, but the product titles end with different random alphanumeric tracking codes.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Manufacturer Part Number ('W6-PH') and Size ('26"&18"') match exactly. However, the titles end differently ('WW826A' vs 'F0828').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical sets of wiper blades with the same dimensions printed on the packaging or item.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore varying alphanumeric codes at the very end of the product title if the structured 'Manufacturer Part Number' and 'Size' attributes match perfectly. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | OneSize:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Minor phrasing differences in Product Name

scenarios.description: Costume sets have identically populated 'One Size' attributes and matching images, despite minor semantic variations in the product title.

conditions.textual_evidence: 'Clothing Size' is explicitly set to 'One Size' for both items. Titles describe the same Rio Bird belly dance skirt set with slight phrasing differences.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images display the exact same outfit (e.g., turquoise sequin fringe crop top and skirt set).

resolution.actionable_instruction: For costume sets, if the 'Clothing Size' attribute identically states 'One Size' and the visual images confirm the same outfit, mark as 'Duplicate', ignoring minor title phrasing variations.

sop_title: Duplicate | Toys & Games | Color Size:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Conflicting color in Main Image URL

scenarios.description: The product images show different colors (e.g., Green vs Blue), but both the Product Name and the 'Actual Color' attribute explicitly state the same color (e.g., Green).

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name and the 'Actual Color' attribute both explicitly indicate 'Green' for both GTINs.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the same exact model of the product, despite displaying different colors.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If both the title and the actual color attribute column agree on a color, override the conflicting color shown in the image and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Gift info :

scenarios.description: One product's main image includes a promotional 'free gift' graphic, while the other products do not, and the text does not mention the gift.

conditions.textual_evidence: The core product details in the Product Name (e.g., Betty Crocker Sugar Cookie Mix 17.5oz) match perfectly without mentioning the gift.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The core product shown in the images is identical; the only difference is the added 'free gift' badge in one image.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore free gift promotional graphics present only in the images. If the core product details and textual descriptions match, mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Rings | Birthstone :

scenarios.description: The 'Birthstone' tabular attribute is missing for one item, but the specific birthstone name is explicitly included in the Product Name for both items.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name string contains the exact birthstone name (e.g., 'Peridot') for both GTINs, compensating for the missing tabular attribute.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show identical rings with the same colored stone.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the Birthstone attribute is missing in the column but is explicitly available and matches in the title, ignore the missing column data and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Kitchen | Size:

scenarios.description: For cups or bowls, one GTIN provides physical dimensions (e.g., in a secondary image) while the other provides volume capacity (e.g., 300ml in description), lacking a direct 1:1 metric comparison.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Names identify the exact same item (e.g., '50pcs Creative Printing Ice Cream Paper Bowls').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images display identically patterned and shaped disposable bowls.

resolution.actionable_instruction: In cups/bowls, if one item provides dimensions and the other provides ml volume information, and all other visual and textual attributes match, treat the size info as compatible and mark as Duplicate.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Package Include :

scenarios.description: The 'Package include' text in the description contains a clearly erroneous, unrealistic quantity (e.g., '2325PC Coat') compared to the standard quantity (e.g., '1 PC Coat').

conditions.textual_evidence: The text contains a clear typo for package inclusion that defies logic for a single retail GTIN.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images show the exact same single clothing item.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore obvious typographical errors or highly unrealistic quantities in 'Package include' text. Mark as Duplicate if remaining attributes and images match.

sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Size :

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Slight formatting variations in age-based sizes

scenarios.description: Apparel items have slightly different age-based size formats where one is a specific year and the other is a range encompassing that year.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Clothing Size' attribute shows '7 Years' for one item and '7-8 Years' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images must show the exact same jacket design and color.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat overlapping or slightly differently formatted age-based sizes (e.g., '7 Years' vs '7-8 Years') as matching values. If images and other core details match, mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Home & Improvement | Bases on Capacity :

scenarios.description: One product has explicit physical dimensions in the 'Size' attribute while the other has a null value, but both explicitly state an identical volume/capacity in the title and description.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles mention '10 Liters' and '10 LT'. Descriptions confirm 'holds up to 10 liters'. The 'Size' attribute has '13" x 13" x 9.9"' for one and is null ('-') for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images confirm both items are the exact same collapsible bucket.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore missing physical dimensions in the 'Size' attribute if the volume/capacity (e.g., '10 Liters', '10 LT') is explicitly stated and matches in the product titles or descriptions. Mark as 'Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Fashion Others | Based on Color:

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Visual similarity in Main Image URL

scenarios.description: Products appear visually identical in the thumbnail images, but the product titles explicitly call out different specific color shades.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title explicitly states 'Aesthetic Ultramarine Blue' while the other states 'Aesthetic Night Blue'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may look identical, but textual evidence of different colors overrides visual similarity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Do not merge items if the product titles explicitly state different specific color names (e.g., 'Ultramarine Blue' vs 'Night Blue'), even if the thumbnail images appear visually identical. Mark as 'Not a Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Fashion | Different Size Format:

scenarios.description: Products have identical images, but their size attributes utilize completely different, non-comparable formatting systems (numeric vs. alphanumeric) without an equivalency reference.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Bra Size' attribute shows '38' for one item and '2XL' for the other. There is no size chart reference to confirm they are equivalent.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical, but cannot resolve the strict size format mismatch.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Flag as 'Not a Duplicate' when sizes use completely different, non-comparable formats (e.g., numeric '38' vs alpha '2XL') and there is no explicit equivalency reference available.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Garden & Patio | Bulbs Different :

scenarios.description: Products have identical titles and images, but a specific attribute ('Character') reveals a difference in the item quantity (bulb count).

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Character' attribute specifies '15bulbs' for one item and '8bulbs' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical placeholders of the bloomed flower, but text dictates the differing pack size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat differences in bulb count/quantity (e.g., found in the 'Character' attribute) as distinct products. If bulb counts mismatch, mark as 'Not a Duplicate', regardless of matching titles and images.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Food & Beverages | Size / No size:

scenarios.description: The pack quantity explicitly stated in the product title differs between the items.

conditions.textual_evidence: One product title contains an extra pack multiplier (e.g., '- Pack of 2') that is missing in the other title.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may look identical, but the text-based pack quantity dictates the difference.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the pack quantity explicitly differs in the product title strings, mark the items as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Beauty | Count Different :

scenarios.description: The 'Count Per Pack' tabular attribute contains explicitly different numerical values.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Count Per Pack' attribute shows mismatched numbers (e.g., 10 vs 1|1) across the compared items.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual evidence is secondary; the explicit attribute difference drives the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If there is a clear difference in the 'Count Per Pack' attribute values, mark the products as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Beds | Size Info:

scenarios.description: The tabular 'Size' attribute is completely missing or null, but the secondary images clearly display different dimensional measurements.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product titles may indicate varying sizes (e.g., King vs California King).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Secondary images explicitly show dimensional drawings with different measurements (e.g., 89 inch vs 91 inch length).

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the size attribute is blank, check secondary images for dimensional diagrams. If these secondary images display different size values, mark as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Fashion | Based on Images:

scenarios.description: The primary product images reveal distinct visual differences in the core design, such as different printed text or logos on the clothing.

conditions.textual_evidence: Textual attributes might contain matching but incorrect/junk data (e.g., 'Sleeveless' for a hoodie), but the primary differentiator is the image.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The main product images clearly show different designs or text printed on the garment (e.g., 'HOUSTON' vs 'BOSTON').

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the primary images show distinctly different product designs or printed text, ignore conflicting or matching tabular attributes like 'Sleeve Length Style' and mark as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Beauty | Code Difference :

scenarios.description: Beauty products (like foundation or tint) have different shade codes embedded in their titles or secondary images, representing distinct color variants.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name explicitly lists different shade numerical codes (e.g., 102 vs 368 vs 372 vs 310).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Secondary images visually confirm different skin tint shades and often display the specific shade code on the swatch or model.

resolution.actionable_instruction: In beauty products, carefully check for shade codes in titles and images. If the shade codes differ, they represent different colors; mark them as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Electronics | Model Difference :

scenarios.description: Products appear visually identical and share the same core specifications (65W AC Adapter), but the specific alphanumeric model numbers embedded in the title and description differ.

conditions.textual_evidence: The titles contain differing model strings in parentheses: '(E0J70UA)' vs '(E8B87UA)' vs '(E9G69UA)' vs '(T0D96UA)'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images look like identical generic black chargers, but textual model numbers govern the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For electronics like laptop chargers, strictly consider the specific model numbers found in the Product Name or Description. If the model numbers differ, mark them as 'Not a Duplicate', regardless of visual similarity.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Caffeine Difference :

scenarios.description: Products share identical packaging images and titles, but their structured 'Caffeine Designation' attribute reveals a crucial product formulation difference.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Caffeine Designation' attribute explicitly states 'Naturally Decaffeinated' for one and 'Naturally Caffeinated' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical (Lipton Diet Peach Iced Tea 12-pack), meaning the visual asset is likely a generic placeholder that doesn't reflect the caffeine difference.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Prioritize the 'Caffeine Designation' attribute for food/beverage items. If one is caffeinated and the other is decaffeinated, flag as 'Not a Duplicate', even if titles and images perfectly match.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate - Compactability | Vehicles, Parts & Accessories | Model Difference

scenarios.description: Products are visually identical sets of brake pads, but the titles explicitly list different, non-overlapping specific years representing vehicle compatibility.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles start with different specific years: '1997 fits...', '1994 fits...', and '1996 fits...'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical stock photos of brake pads, but the textual compatibility years dictate the item.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For vehicle parts, treat specific compatibility years listed in the title or description as distinct models. If the compatibility years differ (and are not listed as an inclusive range), mark as 'Not a Duplicate - Compatibility'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Books | Condition :

scenarios.description: Both books are pre-owned with identical titles and covers, but they differ in their specific sub-condition grades.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Condition' attribute shows 'Pre-Owned: Like New' for one and 'Pre-Owned: Good' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show the identical red book cover.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Do not merge used items with differing sub-conditions. If the 'Condition' attributes show different grades of pre-owned status (e.g., 'Like New' vs 'Good'), mark the products as 'Not a Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Electronics | Versions Different:

scenarios.description: Products are the same core model and visually identical, but one is explicitly designated as a specific regional/market version in the title and description.

conditions.textual_evidence: One Product Name appends '(International Version)' at the end, while the other does not. The description also confirms 'Item Version - International Version'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical stock photos of black Sony headphones.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat explicit regional or market version differences (e.g., 'International Version') as mandatory distinguishing attributes for electronics. If one item has this version designation and the other doesn't, mark as 'Not a Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Video Games | Versions Different:

scenarios.description: The product titles specify different regional or language versions of the same video game.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name contains differing version indicators (e.g., 'EU/PEGI Version', 'Germany Version', 'Japanese Version').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Not strictly required if titles explicitly state different regions, but box art ratings (PEGI, CERO, USK) might visually confirm the region.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the product title specifies different language or regional versions for a video game, treat it as a mandatory difference and mark as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Video Games | Condition Different :

scenarios.description: The condition attribute explicitly shows different states for the item, such as 'Used' versus 'Refurbished'.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Condition' attribute column contains mismatching values (e.g., 'Used' vs 'Refurbished').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual evidence is secondary; the explicit attribute difference drives the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If there is a difference in the condition attributes (e.g., Used vs Refurbished), consider them as different products and mark as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Books | Format Difference:

scenarios.description: The Edition attribute indicates different formats or versions for an audiobook or book.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Edition' column contains conflicting values denoting different formats (e.g., 'CD' vs 'Unabridged').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual evidence is secondary; the explicit attribute difference drives the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the Edition attribute specifies different formats or versions (like CD vs Unabridged) for books or audiobooks, mark the items as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Books | Edition different :

scenarios.description: The Edition attribute contains explicitly different values indicating different publication runs.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Edition' column shows mismatching values (e.g., 'Reprint' vs 'Standard Edition').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual evidence is secondary; the explicit attribute difference drives the decision.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the Edition attribute shows different values (such as Reprint vs Standard Edition), mark the products as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Electronics | Seller Different:

scenarios.description: The product description explicitly states that the electronics have been custom-upgraded or modified by different third-party sellers.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Product Short Description' indicates different sellers providing custom upgrades (e.g., 'Velztorm PC sells computers...' vs 'MichaelElectronics2 sells computers...').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical base laptops, but the text dictates they are different seller-modified SKUs.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If the product descriptions reveal that the items are custom-modified configurations sold by different third-party sellers (different seller warranty/upgrade info), mark them as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Electronics | Model Different:

scenarios.description: Products share identical names, descriptions, and images, but have distinct alphanumeric model numbers listed in their structured attributes.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Model' attribute explicitly shows 'MYH62LL/A' for GTIN 1 and 'MYHX2LL/A' for GTIN 2.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical iPads, but the textual model differences override visual similarity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For electronics, strictly evaluate the 'Model' attribute. If the specific alphanumeric model numbers differ (e.g., 'MYH62LL/A' vs 'MYHX2LL/A'), mark the items as 'Not a Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Shelf Life Different, 2x Pack :

scenarios.description: One product title contains a specific multipack prefix indicator, and the items also differ in their shelf life attribute, despite identical images.

conditions.textual_evidence: The Product Name for GTIN 2 includes the prefix '2X - ', indicating a 2-pack, whereas GTIN 1 is a 1-pack. Additionally, 'fc shelf life' differs (365 vs 990).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images display the exact same single bottle of Badia seasoning, but the title dictates the pack size difference.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Do not merge items if the product title contains explicit multipack indicators (e.g., '2X - ') that differ from the other item. Mark as 'Not a Duplicate'.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Gift Cards | Different Formats

scenarios.description: Gift cards share identical brand names, designs, and values, but they are delivered via completely different fulfillment formats.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Gift Card Format' attribute specifies 'E-Card' for one item and 'Physical' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical 'Happy Birthday' Walmart gift card designs.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Flag as 'Not a Duplicate' if the 'Gift Card Format' attribute differs (e.g., 'E-Card' vs 'Physical'). Different fulfillment methods represent distinct products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Jewelry | Gemstone Type :

scenarios.description: Jewelry items have perfectly matching titles, images, and metal types, but they are crafted with fundamentally different gemstone materials.

conditions.textual_evidence: The 'Gemstone Type' attribute explicitly states 'Cubic Zirconia' for one item and 'Moissanite' for the other.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images show identical engagement ring designs, but the material dictates the product identity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For jewelry, strictly evaluate the 'Gemstone Type'. If the gemstone materials differ (e.g., 'Cubic Zirconia' vs 'Moissanite'), mark the items as 'Not a Duplicate', regardless of identical visual or title information.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Electronics | Pack Different

scenarios.description: Two handheld fan listings represent different product bundles because the items included in the package are not the same.

conditions.textual_evidence: One package includes '1 x Fan (With battery) + 1 x USB data cable', while the other includes '1 x Fan (With battery) + 1 x USB data cable + 1 x base'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: No image validation is required when package contents explicitly differ.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat listings with different included accessories or bundle contents as separate products, even when the base product is identical.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Animal | Pack

scenarios.description: The same catnip product is sold in different pack quantities, indicated by a quantity multiplier in the title.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title shows a single unit '1 oz (28 gram)', while another shows '3X Size' and another shows '2X Size', indicating multiple units or larger pack quantity.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may appear identical because the product is the same brand and item; pack quantity must be determined from the title.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Always consider pack multipliers such as 2X, 3X, 4X, multipack indicators, or quantity information in the title. Different pack quantities are separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Beauty | Pack

scenarios.description: Two Dove soap listings represent different pack configurations even though the soap variant is identical.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title contains '(4 Pack of 2 Bars)' while the other contains '(4 Pack of 4 Bars)'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Packaging images may look similar; the determining factor is the pack-count information in the title.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Use pack quantity stated in the title as a primary differentiator. Different numbers of bars or units indicate different products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Fashion | Size Pattern

scenarios.description: Two pajama listings have the same design and product identity but represent different sizes.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing is size 'XL (160-165 cm)' and the other is size '14 (125-135 cm)'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images can be identical because size variants often share the same product image.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat size information as a differentiating attribute. Different apparel sizes correspond to different sellable variants.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Clothing | Product Type Different

scenarios.description: Listings represent different apparel configurations of the same design, such as shorts only, shirt only, or a shirt-and-shorts set.

conditions.textual_evidence: The highlighted attribute shows values such as 'Only Shorts', 'Shirt+Shorts Set', and 'Only Shirt'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify which clothing pieces are included in the listing and ensure the apparel composition matches the stated variant.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Use actual included apparel pieces as a critical matching attribute. Single-item variants and multi-piece sets must be treated as separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Home Improvement | Actual Color vs Color

scenarios.description: The Actual Color attribute indicates a color assortment/bulk color variant while the Color attribute indicates a single color. These represent different sellable variants.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing contains Actual Color = 'Pink, Purple, Teal' while another contains Actual Color = 'Purple'. The Color field also differs ('Pink' vs 'Purple').
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may appear similar because assortment products often reuse the same product image.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When Actual Color represents a multi-color assortment/bulk variant and another listing represents a specific single-color variant, treat them as separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Books | Language

scenarios.description: The same book is available in different languages or translations.

conditions.textual_evidence: Language attribute differs ('PT' / Portuguese versus 'Chinese'). The title also references different language editions.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Review title text and cover content when available to confirm language differences.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat different language editions, translated versions, or multilingual variants as separate products even if the edition and format are identical.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Rings | Model

scenarios.description: Two rings have the same size and general appearance but the model numbers encode different gemstone or diamond specifications.

conditions.textual_evidence: Model values differ and contain different encoded specifications related to diamond purity, clarity, color, or stone configuration.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Visual similarity should not override model-based gemstone specification differences.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For rings, treat model-number differences that reflect diamond purity, color, clarity, stone composition, or other jewelry specifications as distinct products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Automotive Parts | Tire Season

scenarios.description: Two tires share the same dimensions and specifications but are designed for different seasonal usage.

conditions.textual_evidence: Tire Season differs ('Summer' versus 'All-Season') while other tire specifications remain the same.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Season classification is sufficient; tread images do not need to be evaluated when season data is available.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat Tire Season as a critical differentiator. Summer, Winter, All-Season, and similar seasonal classifications must be considered separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Garden Tools | Model#

scenarios.description: Two garden tools have the same product name and appearance but different manufacturer-assigned model numbers.

conditions.textual_evidence: Model values differ (e.g., GR84769 versus GR796878). Manufacturer Part Numbers also differ.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Do not rely on identical images when model and MPN identifiers are different.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat differing model numbers or MPNs as distinct products unless there is explicit evidence that one identifier is an alias of the other.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Toys & Games | Trading Cards

scenarios.description: Two trading cards have the same card name and card number but one listing contains the keyword 'FOIL', indicating a special card variant.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title contains 'FOIL' while the other title does not contain 'FOIL'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The foil effect may not be visible in product images; rely primarily on title information.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat FOIL and non-FOIL trading cards as different products even when the card name, card number, and artwork are identical.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Toys & Games | Version

scenarios.description: The same video game is sold as different regional or release versions.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title contains a version identifier such as 'US Version' while the other does not.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Box art may appear nearly identical; version information should be determined from the title.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat different regional editions, platform versions, or explicitly stated game versions as separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Compatibility | Battery | Compatibility Model

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Battery model number when compatibility differs

scenarios.description: Multiple battery listings use the same battery model but are marketed for different vehicle makes, models, or year ranges.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles reference different vehicle compatibility such as BMW 3 Series, Chevy Equinox, or Nissan Pathfinder.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Packaging and battery appearance may be identical because the same battery type is used.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For compatibility-based automotive products, use the vehicle compatibility stated in the title as the primary matching criterion. Different compatible vehicle models or year ranges indicate separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Home & Kitchen | Count

scenarios.description: Two listings represent different quantities of the same utensil despite sharing the same multipack quantity.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing has Count Per Pack = 12 while the other listing has no count specified or represents a single unit. Multipack Quantity remains 1 for both.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Identical spoon images do not indicate identical quantity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Use Count Per Pack as a differentiating attribute. A 12-count package and a single-item package must be treated as separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Size

scenarios.description: Two coffee products share the same blend name but differ in package size and/or coffee form.

conditions.textual_evidence: One size is listed as '5 LB-Standard' while the other is listed as '1 LB-Whole Bean'.
