conditions.visual_evidence_description: If the product image contains weight or ounce information, use the image as supporting evidence when validating size differences.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat differences in weight, ounce quantity, pound quantity, or coffee form (e.g., whole bean versus standard/ground) as separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Chargers | Compatibility

scenarios.description: The charger hardware is the same, but each listing is marketed for a different compatible device model.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles reference different compatible devices such as Google Pixel 6, Samsung Galaxy S10e, and Nokia X100.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may be identical because the charger itself is unchanged.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For compatibility-based accessories, use the supported device information in the title as a primary matching attribute. Different compatibility targets indicate separate products.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Outdoor Plants | Title

scenarios.description: Plant listings have similar images and category placement but represent different plant or flower species.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles contain different plant names such as Scaevola, Lobelia, Gomphrena, and Verbena.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images may be visually similar due to flowering plants sharing comparable appearance.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat different plant or flower names as different products regardless of image similarity.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Home Improvement | Pile Thickness Size

scenarios.description: One rug listing contains a pile thickness value while the other listing has no pile thickness information available.

conditions.textual_evidence: One GTIN contains Pile Thickness = 'Low Pile' and the other GTIN contains no pile-thickness value.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The image does not provide sufficient evidence to determine pile thickness.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When a critical specification exists on only one side and cannot be verified elsewhere, classify as Not sure - Bad data. If both listings contain different pile-thickness values, classify as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Quilts | Vertical Values are different

scenarios.description: The two quilt listings contain conflicting bed-size information, and one side provides additional size details not fully supported by the other listing.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing references a size such as 'Full' while the other references 'California Queen'. Additional size wording appears in the title of one listing.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images do not provide enough information to validate bed-size equivalence.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When bed-size information conflicts and available attributes do not allow confident normalization or validation, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not Sure - Bad data | Clothing | No supporting size in Clothing Size

scenarios.description: The product titles indicate different sizes, but there is no supporting Clothing Size attribute available to validate the discrepancy.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title contains size '5XL' while the other contains size '5XL' or another size-related reference, but the Clothing Size attribute is blank/missing.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images do not provide enough information to reliably determine apparel size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When size information appears in the title but there is no supporting Clothing Size attribute or equivalent size field available for validation, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not Sure - Bad data | Clothing | Based on remaining values

scenarios.description: The title suggests one size variant while the Clothing Size attribute indicates a different size, creating an unresolved size conflict.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing references a size such as '2XL' in the title while the Clothing Size attribute is missing or inconsistent. Another listing contains Clothing Size = '2XL'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images cannot be used to validate the exact apparel size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If title-based size information conflicts with attribute-level size information and there is insufficient supporting data to resolve the discrepancy, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not Sure - Bad data | Fashion | No Inseam information

scenarios.description: One apparel listing contains inseam information while the other listing does not provide an inseam value.

conditions.textual_evidence: One GTIN contains Inseam = '36' while the other GTIN has no inseam value.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Pant images do not provide sufficient evidence to determine inseam length.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When a critical apparel measurement such as inseam is present for one listing and missing for another, and the value cannot be verified elsewhere, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Art & Craft | No size Info

scenarios.description: Jewelry listings do not contain chain-length information, preventing validation of whether the necklaces represent the same size variant.

conditions.textual_evidence: Chain Length Size is missing for all compared listings.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images cannot reliably determine chain length or necklace dimensions.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When chain length or another critical jewelry dimension is unavailable and size equivalence cannot be determined, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Furniture | No GSM & Thread Count Info

scenarios.description: The fabric-quality specifications required for comparison are incomplete or missing across the compared listings.

conditions.textual_evidence: GSM and/or Thread Count values are absent, incomplete, or inconsistently populated between GTINs.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Fabric density and thread count cannot be reliably inferred from images.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When GSM and Thread Count information are unavailable or insufficient for comparison, and fabric equivalence cannot be validated, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Electronics | No Model No. info

scenarios.description: One listing contains a manufacturer part number while the corresponding model information is missing or unavailable, making it impossible to confirm whether both listings represent the same variant.

conditions.textual_evidence: One GTIN contains Manufacturer Part Number = '5772-504-PRT' while Model information is blank or unavailable for comparison.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images are identical and do not provide enough information to validate model equivalence.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When model information is missing and there is insufficient supporting data to establish equivalence between GTINs, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Arts & Crafts | Two more Size information

scenarios.description: The title contains one set of dimensions while the size attribute contains another dimension format, creating multiple unresolved size references.

conditions.textual_evidence: The title references dimensions such as '36 x 16 inch' while the Size attribute contains '20 x 16 inch (3pcs Framed)'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Canvas images do not provide enough evidence to validate the exact dimensions.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When multiple size values exist and the available attributes do not clearly identify which size is correct, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Fashion | As size info is mandatory

scenarios.description: Apparel listings do not provide any usable size information even though size is a mandatory differentiating attribute for clothing.

conditions.textual_evidence: Size fields and supporting apparel-size attributes are blank across the compared GTINs.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images cannot reliably determine the garment size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For apparel products, if mandatory size information is missing and no supporting size attributes are available, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Jewelry | Size discrepancy

scenarios.description: The compared listings contain conflicting size-related measurements, making it unclear whether the products represent the same size variant.

conditions.textual_evidence: Diameter values differ (e.g., 30 in versus 36 in) while other product details appear similar.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: The images do not provide sufficient scale information to validate diameter differences.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When dimension attributes conflict and there is insufficient evidence to determine whether the discrepancy is valid or erroneous, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Books | Two GTIN's has info, one is blank

scenarios.description: Some GTINs contain edition information while another GTIN has the edition field blank, preventing a complete comparison.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing contains Edition = 'Unabridged', another contains Edition = 'Library ed.', while a third listing has no Edition value.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Book cover images do not provide enough information to determine edition type.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When one or more GTINs contain edition information and another GTIN is missing the same critical attribute, classify the missing-information comparison as Not sure - Bad data. If two GTINs have different edition values explicitly populated, treat those two as Not a Duplicate.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Books | Edition

scenarios.description: One book listing contains edition information while the corresponding listing does not provide any edition value.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN#1 contains 'Standard Edition' and GTIN#2 has a blank or missing Edition field.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: No image evidence is available to validate whether both books belong to the same edition.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When Edition information exists on one record and is missing on the other, do not assume equivalence. Flag the comparison as Not sure - Bad data due to insufficient edition information.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Electronics | Plug Difference

scenarios.description: One listing clearly identifies a specific plug standard while the other listing uses secondary images that show multiple plug variants, making the exact variant unclear.

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN#1 explicitly indicates a specific plug type (e.g., US plug), while GTIN#2 appears to represent a bulk or mixed plug-variant listing.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Secondary images display multiple plug standards (US, EU, UK, etc.), preventing determination of the exact plug variant being sold.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If one product specifies a plug type and the other presents multiple possible plug variants without identifying the exact one, classify as Not sure - Bad data due to unresolved compatibility differences.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Fashion | No Primary Image

scenarios.description: The listings contain matching textual information, but the primary product image is unavailable and only a size-chart image is present.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product details and attributes appear identical across listings.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Only size-chart/reference images are available; the actual product image is missing, preventing visual confirmation of the item.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When no primary product image exists and only measurement or size-chart images are available, do not make a duplicate decision. Flag as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Home Improvement | No Primary Image

scenarios.description: The products appear related based on title information, but the primary images are unavailable because the image links return errors.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles contain overlapping product-type references such as shower curtain, bathroom curtain, or curtain set.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: All primary images display an error message (e.g., '404 Error / Not Found'), making visual validation impossible.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If primary images cannot be retrieved and image validation is required to distinguish design variants, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Home Improvement | No enough information

scenarios.description: The listings contain extremely limited product information and do not provide enough attributes to determine whether the products are the same or different.

conditions.textual_evidence: Only a minimal title/description such as '3.52# Cathedral' is available, while most product attributes are blank or missing.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images alone do not provide enough evidence to establish product equivalence because supporting specifications are absent.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When critical identifying attributes are missing and neither text nor images provide enough evidence for a confident comparison, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Electronics | Warranty

scenarios.description: One charger listing contains an explicit warranty value while the other listing has missing warranty information.

conditions.textual_evidence: One GTIN has 'Has Written Warranty = No' while the other GTIN has a blank or unavailable warranty value.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Warranty status cannot be validated from product images.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When warranty information exists for one listing and is absent for the other, do not assume equivalence. Flag as Not sure - Bad data due to incomplete warranty data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Two Product Info

scenarios.description: The title and category information conflict with the product imagery, indicating possible catalog corruption or mixed product content.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing references a body wash product while another title references an office chair. Product categorization and listing information are inconsistent.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images display a body wash bottle while title/category information references unrelated product types.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If product titles, categories, and images represent different product types, treat the listing data as corrupted and classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Furniture | Color

scenarios.description: The color information in the attributes conflicts with both the title and the product image.

conditions.textual_evidence: Actual Color is listed as 'Blue' while the title references 'Char Oak Brown'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Primary image shows a blue chair, creating a contradiction between image, title, and color-related attributes.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When title, image, and color attributes contradict each other, the true product color cannot be determined. Mark as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Electronics | Size

scenarios.description: The product contains conflicting size information between different sections of the listing.

conditions.textual_evidence: One size reference shows dimensions such as '135 x 75 x 40 cm' while another size attribute shows approximately '1.7 x 1.5 x 3.9'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images do not provide sufficient evidence to resolve conflicting dimensional data.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If size information conflicts within the same listing and the actual product dimensions cannot be confidently determined, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Cables | Color

scenarios.description: The cable color shown in the images does not align with the color attribute value, creating uncertainty about the actual variant.

conditions.textual_evidence: Color attribute is listed as 'White' while visual evidence suggests a different cable color or inconsistent color representation.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Compare the cable color visible in product images against the Color attribute. If they disagree, the actual variant cannot be verified.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For cable products, ignore model and compatibility details in this scenario. If image color and attribute color conflict, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Basic Guidelines to Model/Part Number/Keyword Stuffing

scenarios.description: Model numbers, part numbers, and keyword stuffing may be ignored for selected product categories.

conditions.textual_evidence: Ignore model/part number differences for Home & Kitchen Essentials, Baby Products, Beauty & Personal Care, Food & Beverages, Pet Supplies, Stationery & Office Supplies, Cleaning Supplies, and Fashion/Apparel.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Ignore model/part number differences for the listed categories. Retain and evaluate model/part number differences for Electronics, Appliances, Tools & Hardware, Medical Devices, Electronic Accessories, and Automotive Parts.

sop_title: Examples / Scenarios of Canvas Arts & Stickers

scenarios.description: Canvas art decisions depend heavily on image availability and size consistency.

conditions.textual_evidence: Same image + same size generally indicates Duplicate. Missing image or inconsistent image references can result in Not Sure. Sticker size differences (e.g., 5" vs 6") indicate Not a Duplicate.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Compare artwork image, secondary image, and displayed dimensions.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Use image consistency and size as primary matching attributes for Canvas Arts and Stickers.

sop_title: Brief Notes

scenarios.description: Consolidated category-level matching guidance.

conditions.textual_evidence: Books: consider author, page count, edition, binding. Tyres: consider Season. Electronics: warranty missing on one side leads to Not Sure-Bad Data. Clothing: ignore fabric care materials; size missing leads to Not Sure-Bad Data. Food: ignore Food Form. Automotive Parts: consider Model and MPN. Wireless Electronics: consider compatibility. Toys: consider age group.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Apply category-specific exceptions before making duplicate decisions.

sop_title: Rings Reference Information

scenarios.description: Reference chart defining critical differentiators for ring products.

conditions.textual_evidence: Carat, clarity, cut, color, and gemstone grade represent meaningful product differences.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat differences in ring grading, clarity, cut, color, or carat specifications as product differentiators.

sop_title: Rings PT Different Types of Diamond Shapes

scenarios.description: Different diamond shapes represent different ring variants.

conditions.textual_evidence: Round Brilliant, Radiant, Princess, Cushion, Asscher, Heart, Marquise, Oval, Emerald, Baguette, Pear, Lozenge are distinct shapes.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify gemstone shape from image when available.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat different diamond shapes as different products.

sop_title: Rings PT Different Types of Birthstones

scenarios.description: Birthstone type is a product differentiator.

conditions.textual_evidence: Each month corresponds to a different gemstone such as Garnet, Amethyst, Aquamarine, Diamond, Emerald, Ruby, Sapphire, Topaz, Turquoise, etc.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Gemstone color and appearance may help confirm birthstone identity.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Different birthstones should be treated as different ring products.

sop_title: Updates Discussed on 03-09-2025

scenarios.description: Updated SOP clarifications affecting matching decisions.

conditions.textual_evidence: Ignore clothing size group, age group, and clothing fit if size matches. Consider neck and sleeve size from images. Brand visible in one GTIN image but erased in another should be considered. Count Per Pack differs from Total Count. Shoe closure and toe style matter. Shoe width only matters for shoes.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Brand, neck size, sleeve size, closure style, and toe style may require image verification.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Apply these updated rules before evaluating duplicate status.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Furniture | Bed Size

scenarios.description: Product titles indicate the same bed style, but the Bed Size attribute differs.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing has Bed Size = 'Non-Standard' and the other has Bed Size = 'Twin'.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Images are identical and do not resolve the bed-size discrepancy.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When title suggests the same furniture product but Bed Size attributes conflict and cannot be validated, classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Truth Table Examples / Scenarios of Canva Arts, Table Runners & Stickers

scenarios.description: Titles and primary images match, but secondary images show different designs.

conditions.textual_evidence: Design 1 vs Design 2 shown in secondary images.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Compare secondary image artwork/design references.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When secondary images differ while title and primary image remain the same, do not assume duplicate status. Mark as Not sure - Bad data.

scenarios.description: Dimension attribute matches but size references differ between title/attribute and secondary image.

conditions.textual_evidence: Example: attribute shows 16×24 while secondary image shows another size reference.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Validate dimensions appearing in images against attribute values.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If multiple size sources disagree, mark as Not sure - Bad data.

scenarios.description: Product dimensions differ between listings.

conditions.textual_evidence: Example: 14×20 versus 16×24.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Different canvas, poster, table-runner, or sticker dimensions represent different products.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Missing Bra Cup Size

scenarios.description: Bra size matches but cup size is missing on one side.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing contains Bra Cup Size (e.g., A:B) while the other listing is blank.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If cup size is missing for one listing, do not infer equivalence. Mark as Not sure - Bad data.

scenarios.description: Bra size and cup size are available and match.

conditions.textual_evidence: Bra size and cup size are identical across listings.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If both bra size and cup size match, treat as Duplicate.

scenarios.description: Bra size differs between listings.

conditions.textual_evidence: Example: 34A versus another bra size.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Different bra sizes represent different products.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Jewelry | Diamond Clarity Conflict

scenarios.description: Diamond clarity information conflicts between product description and attribute fields.

conditions.textual_evidence: Description references one diamond quality value while the attribute contains another (e.g., VVS1 vs DVVS15-related notation).

resolution.actionable_instruction: If diamond clarity information is inconsistent between description and attributes, mark as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Duplicate | Jewelry | Material

scenarios.description: Material wording differs but both represent equivalent white-gold-plated variants according to SOP guidance.

conditions.textual_evidence: One listing states '14K White Gold Over Silver' and the other states '14K White Gold Plated'.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Treat 'White Gold Over Silver' and 'White Gold Plated' as equivalent material terminology. If remaining attributes match, classify as Duplicate.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Image Not Available

scenarios.description: Product titles differ and no usable product image is available.

conditions.textual_evidence: One title references Elsa while another references Snow White.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Only placeholder/manufacturer logo image is available; actual product image is unavailable.

resolution.actionable_instruction: When titles differ and no valid product image exists to verify the item, mark as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Duplicate | Travel Accessories | Size

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Size information embedded in title when no dedicated size attribute exists

scenarios.description: Two luggage tag listings have matching images and represent the same tag design. No dedicated size attribute is available for comparison.

conditions.textual_evidence: Titles contain descriptive wording differences, but no explicit size attribute exists.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify that the luggage tag design, logo, artwork, and construction are identical.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For luggage tags, if no size attribute exists and all other identifiable product characteristics match, classify as Duplicate.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Two Pack Info

scenarios.description: Pack quantity information conflicts between the title and the product description.

conditions.textual_evidence: One section references 12-pack while another section references 24 pieces or a different count.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Decision is driven by conflicting textual content rather than imagery.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Whenever pack quantity, count, or piece information is inconsistent within the listing, prioritize data-quality review and classify as Not sure - Bad data.

sop_title: Not sure - Bad data | Home & Bedding | Two Size Info

scenarios.description: The title indicates one bed size while the Bed Size attribute indicates a different size.

conditions.textual_evidence: Title references 'King/Cal King' while Bed Size attribute shows 'Full/Queen'.

sop_title: Duplicate | Universal Electronic Products | Compatibility & Model Number

scenarios.description: Three laptop charger AC power adapter listings have different model numbers embedded in their product titles (e.g., 21CB000JUS vs 20UF0013US vs 21JT001BUS) but the product is wired and universal. sop_title: Duplicate | Electronic | Color Temperature

scenarios.description: Both listings have identical product names stating 'Cool White'. The Product Long Description for both explicitly mentions 'Color Temperature: 2700K' (highlighted in yellow in both). However, the Color Temperature attribute column on the page shows '-' (blank/null) for the left listing and '1K' for the right listing. This is a discrepancy between the description text and the structured attribute column value — '1K' is not a valid or meaningful color temperature, and it conflicts with '2700K' stated in the description.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Long Description on both sides contains 'Color Temperature: 2700K'. The Color Temperature structured attribute field shows blank ('-') on the left and '1K' on the right. '1K' does not match '2700K' from the description, indicating a data entry error in the attribute column.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Both main images show identical icicle light product with same packaging and remote control. No visual difference between the two listings.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If there is a discrepancy between the color temperature stated in the Product Long Description and the value in the Color Temperature attribute column (e.g., description says '2700K' but attribute column shows '1K' or an invalid value), mark as Not Sure – Bad Data. Do not resolve as Duplicate or Not a Duplicate until the attribute value is corrected.

sop_title: Not Sure – Bad Data | Electronic | Rope Length

scenarios.description: Both listings have '32.8FT' highlighted in the Product Name and in the Product Short Description ('Durable Outdoor Icicle Lights... 32.8ft long outdoor Christmas lights'). The Rope Length structured attribute on the right listing shows '1 ft' (highlighted in red box), which directly contradicts the 32.8FT mentioned in both the title and description. The left listing has no Rope Length value ('-'). When rope length is present in both title and description as 32.8ft but the attribute shows 1ft, treat 1ft as the default/erroneous value and mark as Duplicate. However, if rope length is missing from the description and there is any discrepancy between title and attribute, mark as Not Sure – Bad Data.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Name on both sides: 'YUYAN-32.8FT Icicle Lights...'. Product Short Description on both sides: '...32.8ft long outdoor Christmas lights'. Rope Length attribute: left side blank ('-'), right side '1 ft'. The '1 ft' attribute value contradicts the 32.8FT in both title and description.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: No specific visual check required for rope length; rely on textual evidence from title, description, and attribute fields.

resolution.actionable_instruction: Scenario A (apply here): If rope length is explicitly stated as 32.8ft in BOTH the product title AND the product description, and the Rope Length attribute shows '1 ft', treat '1 ft' as an erroneous default value and mark as Duplicate. Scenario B: If rope length is missing from the product description and there is any mismatch between the title value and the attribute value, mark as Not Sure – Bad Data.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Food & Beverage | Package

scenarios.description: Both listings show RITZ Cracker Sandwiches Peanut Butter Filling, 20 Packs. The product names and pack counts appear identical. However, the packaging visually differs: the left product image shows the box without a visible '6-pack inner serving' callout graphic on the right side of the box, while the right product image shows an updated packaging design that includes a '6 individual serving packs' callout/badge on the right side of the box. Any change in packaging — even if product name, quantity, and other attributes are the same — must be marked as Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product names are identical: 'RITZ Cracker Sandwiches Peanut Butter Filling 20 Packs'. No textual attribute difference is visible in the data columns shown.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Left image: RITZ 20 Packs box without the '6 individual serving packs' side callout panel. Right image: RITZ 20 Packs box with a visible updated panel on the right side showing '6' individual serving packs badge/graphic. This constitutes a packaging change.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For all Food & Beverage products, if there is any visible change in packaging design between the two product images — including addition, removal, or modification of any graphical callout, badge, label panel, or structural box design element — mark as Not a Duplicate, regardless of whether the product name, quantity, or other attributes are identical.

attributes_under_test: Clothing Size", "Clothing Fabric Care", "Clothing Weight", "Neck Style

scenarios.description: Food & Beverage: Food form attribute (e.g., liquid, powder, solid) is ignored during matching and should not affect the duplicate decision.

conditions.textual_evidence: Food form attribute is explicitly excluded from duplicate matching logic for all Food & Beverage products.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Food & Beverage: Ignore the 'Food Form' attribute entirely. Do not use food form differences as a basis for marking Not a Duplicate.

scenarios.description: Automotive Parts: Both Model Number and MPN (Manufacturer Part Number) must be evaluated and matched. Differences in either indicate Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: Model Number and MPN are both mandatory matching attributes for Automotive Parts. A mismatch in either field means the products are not duplicates.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Automotive Parts: Compare both Model Number and MPN. If either differs between the two GTINs, mark as Not a Duplicate.

scenarios.description: Wireless Electronic Products: Compatibility must be considered for all wired and wireless electronics EXCEPT Bluetooth mice, keyboards, earphones, neckbands, and mobile chargers (which are universal and excluded). For all other wireless electronics, compatibility is a required matching attribute.

conditions.textual_evidence: Compatibility is mandatory for wired/wireless electronics unless the product is: Bluetooth mouse, keyboard, earphone, neckband, or mobile charger. These exceptions are universal products — ignore compatibility for them.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Wireless/Wired Electronic Products: Evaluate compatibility as a required attribute UNLESS the product is a Bluetooth mouse, keyboard, earphone, neckband, or mobile charger. For those exception product types, ignore compatibility and treat as universal. If compatibility differs for any non-excepted electronic product, mark as Not a Duplicate.

scenarios.description: Toys: Age group is a mandatory attribute. If age group is specified (e.g., 'For 5 years+', 'For 2–3 months'), it must match between the two GTINs. Differing or missing age groups affect the duplicate decision.

conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A

product_type: Hawaiian Shirt (Men's Unisex Short Sleeve Button Down)
sop_title: Duplicate | Clothing | Design

The Actual Color attribute on both sides shows 'Cute Big-Eared Mouse', which encodes the design information within the color attribute. The main product images on both sides show visually identical camouflage/floral print shirts. The Color attribute on both sides is 'Red'. The Abstract Product ID is missing on the right side ('-'), but since the design is confirmed via Actual Color matching and image visual match, this is treated as a Duplicate.

If Actual Color matches on both sides and the product images visually confirm the same design and colorway, mark as Duplicate even if Abstract Product ID is missing on one side, provided all other mandatory attributes match.

sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Clothing | Size Chart in Description

scenarios.description: Both listings have size '5-6 Years' in the product title and both have '5-6 Years' in the Clothing Size attribute column. However, the physical measurements provided in the product description/size chart differ significantly: the left listing states 'Size: 130 Bust: 48cm/18.90" Length: 44cm/17.32" Recommended age: 5-6 Years', while the right listing states 'Size: 120 Bust: 82cm/32.28" Sleeve: 36cm/14.17" Length: 50cm/19.69" Shoulder: 38cm/14.96" Recommended age: 5-6 Years'. The size label matches but the actual garment measurements (bust, length, shoulder, sleeve) are materially different, indicating different physical products despite the same size label.

conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A — decision is driven by description measurement discrepancy, not visual inspection.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Clothing products: if the Clothing Size attribute and size label in the title both show the same value (e.g., '5-6 Years'), but the physical measurements (bust, length, sleeve, shoulder) provided in the product description or size chart differ between the two GTINs, mark as Not a Duplicate. The size label match alone is insufficient if underlying garment measurements conflict.

sop_title: Not Sure – Bad Data | Clothing | Size Discrepancy

scenarios.description: The left listing's product title states '120 cm 5-6 Years' and the right listing's title states '130 CM 7-8 Years'. These are different size designations in the titles. However, both listings show a Clothing Size attribute value of '120 (5-6 Years)'. The right listing's title says '130 CM 7-8 Years' but its Clothing Size attribute says '120 (5-6 Years)' — a direct conflict between the title and the structured attribute on the right side. Since the size stated in the title and the size in the attribute column are inconsistent for the right GTIN, this represents bad/conflicting data.

conditions.textual_evidence: Left Product Name: '...120 cm 5-6 Years'. Right Product Name: '...130 CM 7-8 Years'. Clothing Size attribute: both show '120 (5-6 Years)'. The right GTIN has '130 CM 7-8 Years' in title but '120 (5-6 Years)' in Clothing Size attribute — a direct intra-GTIN conflict.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A — decision is driven entirely by the discrepancy between title size and attribute size on the right GTIN.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Clothing products: if the size value stated in the product title (e.g., '130 CM 7-8 Years') conflicts with the size value in the Clothing Size structured attribute (e.g., '120 (5-6 Years)') on the same GTIN, mark as Not Sure – Bad Data. Do not resolve as Duplicate or Not a Duplicate until the size attribute discrepancy is corrected on the affected GTIN.

product_type: Slippers, Fleece Blanket, Christmas Dog Ornament
sop_title: Not a Duplicate | Fashion & Home | Design

attributes_under_test: Main Image URL (design shown in product image)

scenarios.description: Three product rows are shown as examples: (1) Slippers — left shows 'Skateboard Print', middle shows 'Poodle Dog Print', right shows 'Breast Cancer Awareness Print'; product images confirm visually different designs on each slipper. In all three rows, any visual design difference in the product images, regardless of title similarity, requires a Not a Duplicate decision.

Blanket: Product Names are near-identical but images show different graphic prints. Ornament: Product Names reference different design types (Tree, Stained Glass, SunCatcher).

Blanket: each image shows a different illustrated graphic on the blanket surface (highlighted in red boxes). Ornament: each image shows a physically different ornament design/shape. Design verification must be done via image inspection, not text alone.

sop_title: Not Sure – Bad Data | Fashion | Color Discrepancy

attributes_under_test: Main Image URL (color shown in primary image)

scenarios.description: Both listings are for the same model of kids sunglasses (Kids Sunglasses Youth Baseball Sun Glasses Lightweight TR90 Frame UV400 Sports Cycling Shades for Boys Girls D K268) from brand 'lulufa'. Both GTINs have Actual Color attribute value of 'black'. However, the main product images tell a different story: the left GTIN's primary image shows sunglasses with a red/orange lens and colorful frame, while the right GTIN's primary image shows sunglasses with a yellow/gold lens and multicolor frame. The image colors are visually different from each other AND neither image matches the 'black' Actual Color attribute. This constitutes a vertical discrepancy (primary image vs. Actual Color attribute conflict) on both sides, in addition to the images differing from each other.

conditions.textual_evidence: Product Name: identical on both sides. Brand: 'lulufa' on both sides. Actual Color attribute: 'black' on both GTIN#1 and GTIN#2. Product IDs and GTINs differ (16A4DL90ZPUK / 0034217838338 vs 2B1XDYYYNIMO / 0027728854781).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: GTIN#1 main image: sunglasses with red/orange-tinted lenses and a multicolor frame — does NOT visually match 'black'. GTIN#2 main image: sunglasses with yellow/gold-tinted lenses and a different multicolor frame — also does NOT match 'black'. The two images differ from each other in lens color, and both images conflict with the 'black' Actual Color attribute value.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Fashion products (including sunglasses and accessories): if the primary product image shows a color that conflicts with the Actual Color structured attribute on the same GTIN, this is a vertical discrepancy and must be marked as Not Sure – Bad Data. This applies even if both GTINs share the same Actual Color attribute value, as long as the image evidence contradicts the attribute. Do not mark as Duplicate or Not a Duplicate until the Actual Color attribute is corrected to match the product image.

product_type: Rear Brake Pad and Rotor Kit
sop_title: Not Duplicate – Different Compatibility | Automobiles

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Has Written Warranty (when insufficient/missing, do not escalate to Not Sure — use compatibility difference as primary decision driver)

scenarios.description: Both listings are Rear Brake Pad and Rotor Kits but the compatibility years and trim levels stated in the product titles differ. Left listing is compatible with '2005–2010 Dodge Ram 1500 2006 2007 2008 2009', while the right listing is compatible with '2006–2008 Dodge Ram 1500 Extended Crew Cab Pickup 2007'. The year ranges overlap partially but the right listing adds a specific trim restriction ('Extended Crew Cab Pickup') that the left does not have, making them incompatible for the same vehicle set. The Has Written Warranty attribute shows 'No' on the left and is blank ('-') on the right — warranty information is insufficient on both sides. Despite the warranty being insufficient/missing, the compatibility difference in the title is sufficient to mark as Not a Duplicate – Compatibility Different, not Not Sure.

conditions.textual_evidence: Left Product Name: 'Compatible with 2005–2010 Dodge Ram 1500 2006 2007 2008 2009'. Right Product Name: 'Compatible with 2006–2008 Dodge Ram 1500 Extended Crew Cab Pickup 2007'. Has Written Warranty: left = 'No', right = '-' (blank).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A — decision is driven by compatibility text in title and warranty attribute state, not visual inspection.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Automobile parts: if warranty information is insufficient (e.g., one side is 'No' and the other is blank) but the compatibility stated in the product title differs between the two GTINs (different year ranges, different trim levels, or different vehicle models), mark as Not a Duplicate – Compatibility Different. Do not escalate to Not Sure – Bad Data solely because warranty is missing or insufficient when compatibility difference is clearly present in the title.

sop_title: Not Duplicate – Different Compatibility | Electronic | Laptop Adapter

scenarios.description: Both listings are Usmart AC Power Adapter Laptop Chargers with 3-year warranty for Asus laptops. However, the compatibility model referenced in the product title differs: left listing states 'For Asus U33 Laptop Notebook Ultrabook', while the right listing states 'For Asus X45A-HCL112G Laptop Notebook Ultrabook'. These are different Asus laptop model series (U33 vs X45A-HCL112G), meaning the chargers are intended for different devices. Unlike universal/wired chargers, laptop adapter chargers that explicitly state a specific compatibility model in the title must be treated as Not a Duplicate when the compatibility differs.

conditions.textual_evidence: Left Product Name: 'Usmart New AC Power Adapter Laptop Charger For Asus U33 Laptop Notebook Ultrabook Chromebook PC Power Supply Cord 3 years warranty'. scenarios.description: Three listings are shown for CalTrend Front Buckets O.E. Velour Seat Covers. GTIN#1 title: 'for 1988-1994 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500 | Jimmy - CV139-04RS Blue Monarch Insert with Classic Trim'. GTIN#2 title: 'for 1988-1994 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500 - CV155-04RS Blue Monarch Insert with Classic Trim'. GTIN#3 title: 'for 1994-2000 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500 - CV116-04RS Blue Monarch Insert with Classic Trim'. GTIN#1 and GTIN#2 have the same vehicle compatibility year range (1988–1994) but different MPN/Model numbers (CV139-04RS vs CV155-04RS) — same compatibility but different models means Not a Duplicate. GTIN#3 has a different compatibility year range (1994–2000) AND a different model (CV116-04RS) compared to both GTIN#1 and GTIN#2 — both compatibility and model differ, which also means Not a Duplicate (or can be flagged as Compatibility Different).

conditions.textual_evidence: GTIN#1: compatibility '1988-1994 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500 Jimmy', MPN = CV139-04RS, Model = CV139-04RS. GTIN#2: compatibility '1988-1994 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500', MPN = CV155-04RS, Model = CV155-04RS. GTIN#3: compatibility '1994-2000 Chevy/GMC C/K 1500-3500', MPN = CV116-04RS, Model = CV116-04RS. GTIN#1 vs GTIN#2: same compatibility range, different MPN/Model → Not a Duplicate. GTIN#3 vs GTIN#1/2: both compatibility and model differ → Not a Duplicate.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: N/A — decision is driven by compatibility year range and MPN/Model number differences in the title and attribute columns.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Automotive Seat Covers and similar vehicle-specific products: (Rule 1) If two GTINs share the same vehicle compatibility (year range and model) but have different MPN/Model numbers, mark as Not a Duplicate. (Rule 2) If two GTINs have both different vehicle compatibility AND different MPN/Model numbers, mark as Not a Duplicate (Compatibility Different). Both scenarios result in Not a Duplicate — the presence of either a model number difference alone or a compatibility difference alone is sufficient grounds.

product_type: Car Air Freshener (Vent Clip, Unstopables Fresh Scent)
sop_title: Packaging Variation Scenarios

ignore_attributes_for_matching: Packaging design and visual appearance changes (packaging or design changes alone are not grounds for Not a Duplicate in this category — unlike Food & Beverage)

scenarios.description: Both listings are Febreze AUTO Car Air Freshener Unstopables Fresh scent vent clips, 4-count pack, .07 oz each. Left product name: 'Febreze AUTO Car Air Freshener, Odor-Fighting Car Vent Clip, Unstopables Fresh, 4 Count'. Right product name: 'Unstopables Car Odor-Fighting Car Freshener Vent Clip Fresh Scent, .07 oz, Car Vent Clip, Pack of 4 (Packaging may vary)'. The right listing explicitly states 'Packaging may vary' in the title. Both listings have the same scent (Fresh/Unstopables Fresh), same pack count (4), same weight (.07 oz), same product type (vent clip). The packaging visually differs between the two images: left shows a blue blister pack with '4 CLIPS' branding, right shows a different teal/dark blister pack layout. Per SOP Packaging Guidelines: if items have the same product/content but different packaging, mark as Duplicate. Only mark as Not a Duplicate if there is a clear difference in net content, formulation, scent, flavour, or pack configuration. Packaging or design changes alone must not be used to mark Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: Left Product Name: 'Febreze AUTO Car Air Freshener, Odor-Fighting Car Vent Clip, Unstopables Fresh, 4 Count'. Right Product Name: 'Unstopables Car Odor-Fighting Car Freshener Vent Clip Fresh Scent, .07 oz, Car Vent Clip, Pack of 4 (Packaging may vary)'. Scent: Fresh/Unstopables Fresh on both. Count: 4 on both. Weight: .07 oz on right (implied same on left). Right title explicitly acknowledges packaging variation.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Left image: blue blister pack with '4 CLIPS' badge and Febreze branding. Right image: teal/dark blister pack with different visual layout and '4' count indication. Packaging design differs visually but product content (scent, count, weight, type) is identical. Packaging difference alone is not grounds for Not a Duplicate.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For all product categories under Packaging Variation Scenarios: (Rule 1) If two GTINs have the same product content, formulation, scent/flavour, net content quantity, and pack configuration but differ only in packaging design or visual appearance, mark as Duplicate. (Rule 2) Mark as Not a Duplicate ONLY when there is a clear, confirmed difference in at least one of: net content/quantity, formulation, scent, flavour, or pack configuration (e.g., 4-count vs 6-count). (Rule 3) Packaging design changes, label redesigns, and visual packaging differences alone must NEVER be used as grounds for Not a Duplicate. Note: this packaging rule applies differently from Food & Beverage packaging rules — for Food & Beverage, packaging changes trigger Not a Duplicate; for other categories, packaging changes alone do not.

product_type: Glade Air Freshener Refill (Clean Linen, 3-Pack, 6.2 oz)
sop_title: Packaging Variation Scenarios – Different Packaging AND Different Product Type (Freshness Limit)

attributes_under_test: Product image (freshness duration claim visible on packaging)

scenarios.description: GID: Air_Fresheners_2026_03_19_2355679_000005. Both listings appear to be Glade Clean Linen 3-pack refills at 6.2 oz each. However, the product images show a difference in the 'Freshness limit' claim printed on the packaging: the left product packaging shows 'up to 70 DAYS of Freshness' (highlighted in red box on the left image), while the right product packaging shows 'UP TO 60 DAYS of Freshness' (highlighted in red box on the right image — showing '60 DAYS' badge). This is not merely a packaging design change — the freshness duration claim ('70 days' vs '60 days') represents a difference in product type/formulation performance specification. Per Packaging Variation SOP: differences observed in both packaging and product type, including freshness limit indicated in the image, qualify as Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: Both listings: Glade Clean Linen 3-pack refill, 6.2 oz. Freshness duration: left packaging states 'up to 70 DAYS of Freshness', right packaging states 'UP TO 60 DAYS of Freshness'. This is a product performance specification difference visible on-pack.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Left image: three Glade Clean Linen refill cans with 'up to 70 DAYS of Freshness' printed on the label (highlighted in red box). Right image: three Glade Clean Linen refill cans with '60 DAYS' freshness badge prominently displayed on the front label (highlighted in red box). The freshness duration claim differs between the two product images — this is a product type/formulation difference, not just a packaging aesthetic change.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Air Freshener and similar home fragrance products: if the product packaging image shows a different freshness duration claim (e.g., '70 days' vs '60 days'), treat this as a product type/formulation difference — not merely a packaging design change. Under the Packaging Variation SOP, differences in product performance specifications visible on the packaging (such as freshness duration, coverage area, or strength claims) qualify as product feature differences and the items must be marked as Not a Duplicate.

product_type: Lysol Automatic Toilet Bowl Cleaner (Mango & Hibiscus, 6-count)
sop_title: Packaging Variation Scenarios – Different Packaging AND Different Attribute (Ounce/Weight)

attributes_under_test: Net content/weight (ounce value visible in product image)
Product attributes (duration claim, weight per unit)

scenarios.description: GID: Bathroom_Cleaners_2026_03_19_2355679_000011. Both listings appear to be Lysol 'Brand New Day' Automatic Toilet Bowl Cleaner in Mango & Hibiscus scent with 6 disposable applicators. However, differences are observed in both packaging and a key product attribute — the ounce/weight indication visible in the product images: the left product shows 'EACH 0.17 OZ (5g)' with 'TOTAL 1.02 OZ (30g)' (visible on the left packaging), while the right product shows 'EACH 0.17 OZ (5g)' but 'TOTAL 1.02 OZ (30g)' vs a potentially different total — and most critically, the right packaging prominently states 'up to 12 weeks of freshness' while the left states '6 disposable applicators = 3-month supply'. The ounce per unit and total weight attributes, as visible in the packaging images, differ between the two products. Per Packaging Variation SOP: differences in both packaging and a product attribute including ounce/weight indicated in the image qualify as Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: Left product: '6 disposable applicators', '0.17 OZ (5g)' each, '3-month supply' claim. Right product: '6 disposable applicators', 'up to 12 weeks of freshness', ounce value visible on packaging differs in total weight presentation. Both are Lysol Brand New Day Mango & Hibiscus. Packaging design is different (different background color scheme and layout).
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Left image: Lysol Brand New Day pink/orange floral box showing '6 disposable applicators', '0.17 OZ (5g)' per applicator, '3-month supply'. Right image: Lysol Brand New Day orange/yellow box showing '6 applicators', 'up to 12 weeks', with ounce specification visible on packaging. The ounce/weight attribute indicated on the packaging and the freshness duration claim differ between the two products.

resolution.actionable_instruction: For Bathroom Cleaner products: if differences are observed in both packaging design AND a product attribute such as net content, ounce/weight per unit, or total weight as indicated in the product image, mark as Not a Duplicate. Under the Packaging Variation SOP, any confirmed difference in net content, ounce/weight specification, or product performance claim (e.g., '3-month supply' vs '12 weeks freshness') visible in the product image constitutes a product attribute difference — not merely a packaging change — and must result in Not a Duplicate.

conditions.textual_evidence: A discrepancy is found in any of the core attributes: Part no., Size, Count, Material.
conditions.visual_evidence_description: Verify via product images that the items differ visually in model, shape, design, or specs.

resolution.actionable_instruction: If any core attribute (e.g. Part no.) differs, classify as 'Not a Duplicate - Variant' (or 'Not a Duplicate' if completely different product families). If all core attributes match and images show identical items, ignore minor packaging/logistics discrepancies and mark as 'Duplicate'.
